title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Conflict Triage
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded Git automation and trust policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Safe Directory Ownership evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Safe Directory Ownership assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Safe Directory Ownership has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Safe Directory Ownership precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Adding wildcard safe.directory is the correct fix for every ownership warning.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

stimulus.title: Hook Lifecycle case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Cloning a repository installs and runs all versioned hooks automatically.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects. Artifact under review: a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Hook Lifecycle, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-safe-directory-ownership-1yta6j" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
